mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales as measured by RSAFS signal increasing consumer spending, which drives corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon. This is an always-on trend signal that does not require a threshold condition.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods of rising RSAFS are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0%, the labor market becomes very tight, increasing wage pressure and prompting the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, which depresses equity valuations and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: In periods with UNRATE below 4.0%, the average 20-day forward return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05), or the signal fails to predict negative returns in at least 55% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising industrial production and low federal funds rates indicate a strong economy with accommodative monetary policy, creating a favorable environment for corporate profits and stock prices. The interaction between rising real activity and cheap credit amplifies the positive effect on forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO increase * FEDFUNDS low) in a linear regression does not have a statistically significant positive coefficient (p < 0.10) over the walk-forward period, or combined signal yields Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, borrowing costs become restrictive, compressing corporate profit margins and discounting future cash flows at higher rates, which leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month. This threshold-triggered signal is active only when rates are high.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, the average 20-day forward return is not negative, or less than 55% of such episodes produce negative returns over the walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of rising retail sales and rising inflation signals an overheating economy that will force the Federal Reserve to tighten policy, thereby depressing equity prices. The interaction between accelerating demand and inflationary pressure provides a stronger negative signal than either series alone over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term (RSAFS growth * CPIAUCSL growth) in a regression predicting 20-day returns does not have a significant negative coefficient (p < 0.10), or the combined signal does not improve out-of-sample accuracy over individual series.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production as measured by INDPRO reflects robust manufacturing activity, which translates into higher corporate earnings and economic growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon. This is an always-on trend signal that is continuously active.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or in periods of rising INDPRO the 60-day forward return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During an early-cycle economic recovery characterized by falling unemployment and improving demand, rising housing starts signal strengthening residential investment and consumer confidence, which boosts economic growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declining for 2 consecutive months and below 5.0%
failure_condition: In periods meeting the trigger condition, the average 20-day forward return is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05), or the directional accuracy of rising HOUST predicting positive returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong employment growth combined with an accommodative monetary policy (low fed funds rate) creates a virtuous cycle of rising incomes, consumer spending, and business investment, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon. The interaction between robust labor market conditions and cheap credit amplifies the effect.
failure_condition: The interaction term (PAYEMS growth * inversed FEDFUNDS) does not have a statistically significant positive coefficient (p < 0.10) in predicting 60-day returns, or the combined signal fails to improve over individual signals in walk-forward testing.
